outcome_text: Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties, including relevant AI actors.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI continuous improvement methodology, including integrating measurable improvement activities into AI system update cycles, establishing regular engagement cadences with interested parties and AI actors, and tracking improvement metrics across AI system iterations.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI continuous improvement methodology, including integrating measurable improvement activities into AI system update cycles, establishing regular engagement cadences with interested parties and AI actors, and tracking improvement metrics across AI system iterations.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift detection, performance monitoring, retraining triggers, and decommissioning criteria.
comments: NIST AI RMF MG-4.2 (MANAGE) superset of WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.595, cosine: 0.637). Secondary: RM-T-005 (RM, cosine: 0.634), AG-T-010 (AG, cosine: 0.600).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.595). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [across, activities, actors, cadences, continuous, cycles] vs WIDAI terms [criteria, decommissioning, detection, drift, lifecycle, management] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.889 (P=0.883, R=0.895). Balanced P/R — the 34-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of AI continuous improvement methodology, including integrating measur" and "Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift de". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.637 ≈ STS 0.595 (gap 0.042). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.8%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge]. NIST AI RMF-only: [across, activities, actors, cadences, continuous, cycles]. WIDAI-only: [criteria, decommissioning, detection, drift, lifecycle, management].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.595 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
